skill.name: community-marketing
skill.description: Build and leverage online communities to drive product growth and brand loyalty. Use when the user wants to create a community strategy, grow a Discord or Slack community, manage a forum or subreddit, build brand advocates, increase word-of-mouth, drive community-led growth, engage users post-signup, or turn customers into evangelists. Trigger phrases: "build a community," "community strategy," "Discord community," "Slack community," "community-led growth," "brand advocates," "user community," "forum strategy," "community engagement," "grow our community," "ambassador program," "community flywheel."
skill.summary: Expert guidance for building, launching, and growing online communities that drive product growth and brand loyalty.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: The community-marketing skill is a content-only marketing skill with no executable code. All static analyzer findings are false positives: the 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are triggered by text patterns in the skill description and evals, the 'backtick execution' warnings are from markdown code blocks and file path references, and the 'network reconnaissance' flag is from natural language text. No actual security risk was identified.

content.user_title: Build Thriving Communities That Drive Growth
content.value_statement: Building an engaged community from scratch is hard without a clear strategy. This skill provides expert playbooks for launching, growing, and measuring communities that create member value and drive business results.
actual_capabilities: Diagnose community health using metrics like DAU/MAU ratio, new member post rate, and thread reply rate
Apply playbooks for launching communities from zero with 20-50 founding members
Design new member journeys including welcome posts, introduce-yourself channels, and onboarding sequences
Create brand ambassador programs with candidate identification, benefits design, and measurement plans
Select appropriate platforms (Discord, Slack, Circle, Reddit, Facebook Groups, Discourse) based on audience
Build community-led support systems that reduce ticket volume while maintaining customer satisfaction
limitations: Cannot directly access or manage Discord, Slack, or other community platform APIs
Does not provide real-time community analytics or automated moderation
Guidance is strategic and advisory; execution requires human community managers
Cannot replace dedicated community management tools for scheduling, tracking, and automation

use_cases.title: Launch a New Community from Zero
use_cases.description: Get a step-by-step playbook for recruiting founding members, setting culture, seeding conversations, and defining the core engagement loop for a brand-new community.
use_cases.target_user: Startup founders launching their first community

use_cases.title: Revive a Stagnant Community
use_cases.description: Run a health audit using proven metrics to diagnose why engagement has dropped, then apply targeted tactics to re-activate lurkers and power users.
use_cases.target_user: Community managers dealing with declining engagement

use_cases.title: Design an Ambassador Program
use_cases.description: Identify potential advocates, design meaningful benefits, create outreach templates, and set up measurement systems for a brand ambassador initiative.

prompt_templates.title: Platform Selection for New Community
prompt_templates.prompt: We're a B2B SaaS company considering starting a community. Should we use Discord or Slack? Our audience is product managers at mid-market companies.
prompt_templates.scenario: Choosing the right platform for a professional audience

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose Low Engagement
prompt_templates.prompt: We launched our community 3 weeks ago with 40 members but only 2-3 people post regularly. Everyone else just lurks. What should we do?

prompt_templates.title: Write Community Guidelines
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me write community guidelines for our Discord. We're building a community for indie game developers who want to share works-in-progress and get feedback.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating guidelines that reinforce shared identity

prompt_templates.title: Design Ambassador Program
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an ambassador program for our 5,000-member community. We have several power users who always help others and we want to give them more recognition and structure.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building a formal advocate program from organic champions

output_examples.input: We just launched our community 3 weeks ago with 40 members but only 2-3 people post regularly. What do we do?
output: Diagnosis: Launch-stage new member activation problem. Members joined but aren't transitioning to participants.
Immediate actions: DM every new member personally within 24 hours of joining. Host a weekly 30-minute call to build relationships with the 2-3 active posters.
Welcome journey: Create a pinned welcome post, set up an #introduce-yourself channel, and send a first-week DM template asking one specific question about their work.
Seed conversations: Post 5-10 messages yourself this week modeling the behavior you want — questions, wins, resources.
Core loop definition: Decide on one weekly action (e.g., share what you're working on) and make it easy and rewarded.
Reality check: At this stage, 1% of members will generate 90% of value. Identify and invest in your 2-3 power users before chasing lurkers.

output_examples.input: Help me select a platform. We're a B2B SaaS targeting product managers.
output: Primary recommendation: Slack — familiar to your professional audience, fits their workday context, and aligns with their existing tools.
Key trade-offs: Free tier limits message history to 90 days; the environment can feel like work rather than community.
Consider Discord if: your users are developers or creators who prefer real-time chat and informal tone.
Consider Circle if: you want a clean dedicated experience and are willing to drive all your own traffic (no organic discovery).
Avoid Facebook Groups for this audience — declining organic reach and wrong demographic fit.
Next step: Validate by asking 10 of your target users which platform they already check daily.

best_practices: Build the community around a shared identity, not just your product — members stay for the people, not the tool
Define one core weekly action and make it easy and rewarded to create habit and engagement
Do things that don't scale at launch — DM every new member, welcome by name, and personally seed the first 50 conversations
anti_patterns: Opening the community publicly before having 20-50 engaged founding members who model the behavior you want
Letting company team members dominate posting volume — aim for majority content from members, not staff
Sending generic, form-based asks to potential ambassadors — always make the outreach personal and specific to why you chose that person

faq.question: What is community-led growth?
faq.answer: Community-led growth is a strategy where the community itself becomes a primary driver of acquisition, retention, and product feedback. Members refer others, help onboard new users, and shape the product through collective input.

faq.question: How do I know if my community is healthy?
faq.answer: Track five core metrics weekly: DAU/MAU ratio (above 20% is healthy), new member post rate within 7 days, thread reply rate, lurker ratio after 30 days, and percentage of content created by non-staff members.

faq.question: Should I use Discord or Slack for my community?
faq.answer: Use Slack for B2B and professional communities where your audience already uses it for work. Use Discord for developer, gaming, or creator communities that prefer real-time informal chat. Use Circle for course-based or creator communities wanting a clean dedicated experience.

faq.question: How many founding members do I need before opening publicly?
faq.answer: Recruit 20-50 engaged founding members manually before opening the community publicly. This baseline of activity creates social proof and ensures new joiners see active conversations, not an empty space.

faq.question: What benefits should ambassador program members receive?
faq.answer: Offer meaningful benefits beyond 'early access to features.' Good options include exclusive product access, revenue share, public recognition, swag, direct input on the product roadmap, and a private channel with the founding team.

faq.question: Can community-led support reduce ticket volume?
faq.answer: Yes, when peers are recognized for helping and when community answers become a searchable knowledge base. The key is making peer support feel valued, not used as a way to dodge company responsibility for customer issues.
